evals.prompt: Turn the offline-mode PRD into issues.
evals.expected_output: Activates ghosts: reads the repo envelope and the blueprint PRD, splits the plan into tracer-bullet vertical slices (each cutting through every layer end-to-end and demoable on its own, preferring many thin slices), marks each HITL or AFK, sets dependencies, and publishes Linear issues/sub-issues with save_issue in dependency order (blockers first) with blocked-by relations, labels, project, milestone, and estimate from the envelope. Does not implement and does not rescope the PRD.

Activates ghosts from the casual request: works from the plan/spec in context, breaks it into thin tracer-bullet vertical slices with HITL/AFK marks and dependencies, and stamps them as dependency-ordered Linear issues via save_issue. Does not start writing code.

Despite the typos, activates ghosts: splits the work into dependency-ordered tracer-bullet vertical slices and publishes them as Linear issues/sub-issues with blocked-by relations, without implementing or changing the parent scope.
Does NOT activate ghosts. Recognizes this as executing a single existing issue end-to-end and routes to the roboports skill; ghosts stamps planned work but never implements.

evals.prompt: Sort and prioritize the open issues, mark what's ready to pick up.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT activate ghosts. This is prioritizing/state-triage of issues that already exist, which routes to the inserter skill rather than stamping new ghosts.
